description: Strategy registry for the experiment agent. Each entry is a strategy variant (code change or config preset) with its status and performance data. Version 2: synced statuses with findings.json from 2026-03-30 campaign.

strategies.description: Wide beam + moderate noise. Proven best for n=25 admission rate.
strategies.notes: Dominant config across local and production. 3-7x better than alternatives.

strategies.description: Focused edge flipping with moderate depth.
strategies.notes: Second-best. High discovery count but lower admission rate. Different exploration mode.

strategies.description: Very wide beam, low noise.
strategies.notes: Underperforms wide-a. Too wide without enough noise = less diverse exploration.

strategies.description: Narrow beam, deep search.
strategies.notes: Worst performer. Slow rounds, few discoveries.

strategies.description: Score-aware tabu walk within valid-graph space. Replaces 3-step greedy polish with 100-500 step tabu walk using incremental 4-clique and triangle deltas.

metrics.note: Enabled all workers to find rank-1 quality graphs. Core improvement.

strategies.notes: Highest-ROI code change. Directly targets scoring quality.

strategies.description: Cross-round fingerprint dedup persistence via carry_state.

metrics.note: Prevents re-exploration across rounds. ~15% unique discovery improvement.

strategies.description: Prefer balanced kc/ei in beam selection when violations are low.

metrics.note: Steers beam toward regions producing better-scoring valid graphs.

strategies.description: Iterated Local Search polish: chain multiple tabu walks separated by random valid-preserving perturbations. Escapes local optima that single walks converge to.

metrics.note: Tested mpd=50/ILS=5/500steps for 27+ min AND mpd=0 (unlimited) for 62+ min. Both zero discoveries. Same 4c>=67 barrier.

strategies.notes: Tested on current binary with deep polish configs: (1) mpd=50, ILS=5, 500 steps ran 27+ min, 0 discoveries. (2) mpd=0 (unlimited) ran 62+ min across 2 workers, 0 discoveries. The 4c>=67 barrier is structural regardless of polish depth.

strategies.description: Template enumeration: analyzes structural backbone shared by top-scoring leaderboard graphs, identifies free edges (edges that differ), then exhaustively or randomly enumerates combinations within that constrained space.
strategies.notes: Genuinely untested. Searches BETWEEN known-good solutions by identifying shared backbone + free edges. However, n=25 is structurally exhausted at 4c>=67 (12+ algorithms, 130K+ rounds confirm). Template may also hit this barrier since even from-scratch construction and SA both land in 4c>=67 basin. Low expected value on n=25. Recommended test: 4 workers with --strategy template --n 25 --sample-bias 0.3 --template-pool-size 3 --polish-max-steps 100.

strategies.description: Crossover recombination strategy: combines structural features from two parent graphs.

metrics.note: Tested with shallow polish only. All offspring at 4c>=67.

strategies.notes: Produces valid R(5,5) graphs but all at 4c>=67. Deep repair (50k iters/offspring) didn't help. Tested with shallow polish (max_polish_per_depth=5, ILS=0).

strategies.description: 2-opt paired edge flips in polish for expanded neighborhood.

metrics.note: O(n^4) overhead (~22s/round vs ~1.8s control) for no quality improvement.

strategies.notes: A/B tested with 12 workers. 2-opt adds O(n^4) paired-flip evaluation but produces same skip_thr=200 at 4c>=67. Neighborhood expansion doesn't help.

strategies.description: Simulated Annealing: traverses through invalid states via probabilistic acceptance.

metrics.note: 36.8B SA steps across temps 5-50, zero discoveries.

strategies.notes: Most extensively tested strategy. High-temp SA (temp=50) reaches diverse valid-graph neighborhoods but all share 4c>=67 floor.

strategies.description: Greedy construction from scratch (no Paley seed).

metrics.note: 424M construction attempts, all valid graphs at 4c>=67.

strategies.notes: Key finding: building from scratch proves 4c>=67 is a property of the R(5,5) constraint space on n=25, not the starting point.

strategies.description: Circulant graph C(n,S) exhaustive enumeration.

metrics.note: DEFINITIVE: Zero valid R(5,5) graphs exist among Z_25 circulant graphs.

strategies.notes: Exhaustive search of all 2^12=4096 circulant graphs on n=25. No valid R(5,5) graphs in this algebraic subspace. Definitive — not affected by polish depth.

strategies.description: Large Neighborhood Search: exhaustive Gray code enumeration of edge rewirings within vertex blocks.

metrics.note: Tested block sizes 5/6/7. k=5,6 produce skip_thr=200 at 4c>=67. k=7 too destructive (skip_thr=2).

strategies.notes: Block_size=7 proves wider neighborhoods destroy R(5,5) validity. The 4c>=67 barrier is not a neighborhood radius problem.

strategies.description: Cayley Z_p×Z_p graph exhaustive enumeration.

metrics.note: DEFINITIVE: Zero valid R(5,5) graphs exist among Z_5×Z_5 Cayley graphs.

strategies.notes: Exhaustive search of all 4096 Cayley graphs of Z_5×Z_5. No valid R(5,5) graphs. Definitive — not affected by polish depth.

strategies.description: Path relinking between elite leaderboard graph pairs.
strategies.notes: Corridor walks between elite graph pairs with violation-tolerant tunneling hit same quality floor.

strategies.description: Two-phase gradient descent: directed 4c minimization + tabu repair.
strategies.notes: Phase 1 pushes max(4c) below 67 but phase 2 repair always snaps back to 4c>=67 validity basins.

strategies.description: Seidel switching search: structured multi-edge perturbations.

metrics.note: Switch sizes 2-4 with 5K-20K compositions. Structurally different space but same ceiling.

strategies.notes: Seidel switching explores different valid-graph space (sparser: skip_thr=6-13 vs tree2's 200) but same 4c>=67 quality ceiling.

strategies.description: Elite refinement: deep ILS polish of top leaderboard graphs.

metrics.note: 2000-5000 step ILS walks on 4c=65 graphs. All variants push to 4c>=67.

strategies.notes: The 4c=65 optimum is a fragile island — even a few valid-preserving edge flips push into 4c>=67 basin. noise_flips>0 wastes 40-60% of rounds.

strategies.description: Proven tree2-wide-a config targeting n=35 R(5,5). n=35 leaderboard has wide quality spread (4c 435→462), entirely owned by one key. New target with clear room for improvement.
strategies.notes: Extends proven n=25 wide-a config to n=35. Larger graph (595 edges vs 300) means slower rounds — may need beam_width/depth tuning. Paley init uses p=37 (truncated). Server has 500 n=35 entries to seed from. Target: beat 4c=(462,463) threshold to start admitting, then optimize toward top 4c=(435,443). Run: ./scripts/fleet.sh --workers 6 --n 35 --release --dashboard ws://localhost:4000/ws/worker

strategies.description: Polish seed graphs directly: when init_graph is already valid, run ILS polish on it before beam search. Preserves quality of top-ranked seeds instead of letting beam search diverge.

params.note: Uses existing polish_max_steps/tabu_tenure/ils_restarts params. No new config needed.

strategies.notes: Key for n=35: workers seed from rank #1 (4c=435) but beam search regresses to 4c=460. Seed polish explores the neighborhood of the seed's quality basin via tabu walk. Prior fleet testing used polish_ils_restarts=0 (ILS disabled) so seed-polish was never properly tested. Commit 4ebb61b enables ILS by default (restarts=3). Re-test with 8 workers on n=35 to properly evaluate.

strategies.description: Enable ILS restarts by default (0→3). All workers now do 3 perturbation+re-walk cycles per valid graph, expanding search from N to 4N effective polish steps.
strategies.notes: ILS perturbation was implemented but disabled by default (restarts=0). All fleet workers including 'deep-ils' were running without ILS. This commit enables 3 restarts by default, giving 4x effective polish depth. Deep-polish (500 steps × 6 walks = 3000 effective) and deep-ils (300 steps × 6 walks = 1800 effective) workers now also explicitly pass --polish-ils-restarts 5. Fleet default N changed from 25 to 35. Critical for n=35 where 595-edge space needs wider neighborhood search to close gap from our 4c=(457,456) to competitor's 4c=(435,445).

ideas.description: RESOLVED: Deep ILS tested on current binary. mpd=50/ILS=5/500steps ran 27+ min, mpd=0 ran 62+ min. Both zero discoveries. Barrier is structural.
ideas.rationale: Tested 2026-03-30. See ils-polish strategy entry and findings.json for evidence. The 4c>=67 barrier on n=25 is independent of polish depth.

ideas.description: Test template strategy on n=35 (not n=25 which is exhausted): 4 workers with --strategy template --n 35 --sample-bias 0.3 --template-pool-size 3. n=35 has quality headroom (our 4c=460 vs competitor 4c=435).
ideas.rationale: Template is untested. n=25 is structurally exhausted (4c>=67 barrier confirmed by 12+ strategies). Testing template on n=25 would be wasteful. n=35 has real quality gap to close.

ideas.description: IMPLEMENTED: See tree2-n35-wide-a strategy entry. n=35 leaderboard 500/500, single-key owned, quality spread 4c=(435,443)→(462,463). Binary confirmed working on n=35.
ideas.rationale: n=25 structurally exhausted at 4c>=67. n=35 has 595 edges (larger search space), wide quality spread, and is entirely owned by one key. Paley init uses p=37.

ideas.description: Run up to 8 workers simultaneously (WSL2 16GB limit) with known-best config. Pure throughput play.
ideas.rationale: On saturated leaderboards, more compute = more chances. 8 workers safe on 16GB WSL2 (12 caused OOM).

ideas.description: Use valid Cayley Z_5×Z_5 graphs as alternative seeds for tree2 beam search.
ideas.rationale: ABANDONED: Cayley enumeration found ZERO valid R(5,5) graphs in Z_5×Z_5 space. No seeds available.

ideas.description: Use valid circulant graphs as alternative seeds for tree2.
ideas.rationale: ABANDONED: Circulant enumeration found ZERO valid R(5,5) graphs in Z_25 space. No seeds available.

ideas.description: Dynamically adjust polish_max_steps based on how many valid graphs are found per round.
ideas.rationale: Currently polish depth is fixed. Could balance compute but unlikely to break 4c>=67 barrier.

ideas.description: GPU-accelerated clique counting via CUDA or wgpu.
ideas.rationale: ABANDONED for n=25: violation_delta=33.6ns, GPU launch overhead 150-300x more expensive. Only revisit for n=40+.
